# Prole

> Prole is a free macOS menu bar app. Snip anything on your screen, mark it up (arrows, a pen, and a text tool that writes right on the image), then copy it and paste it into Claude or any app that takes an image. It is a visual "ask about my screen" tool. It captures pixels and your notes only, with no DOM or code extraction. Claude is the main use case, but the copied image is a normal screenshot that works anywhere.

Prole runs on macOS 12 or later (universal, Apple Silicon and Intel). It isn't notarized yet, so the first launch needs a right-click then Open. Download: https://prole.jeffrey.build/download/Prole.dmg

## How it works

1. Hit your hotkey or the menu bar icon, then draw a box around anything on screen.
2. Mark it up with boxes, arrows, and a freehand pen. Use the text tool to write your question right on the image.
3. Hit Copy. The image is on your clipboard. Paste it into Claude, ChatGPT, Slack, email, or any app that takes an image.

## Key facts

- Price: free
- Platform: macOS 12+ (universal: Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Install: download the .dmg, drag Prole to Applications, right-click then Open on first launch, and grant Screen Recording on first capture
- What it captures: screen pixels plus any notes you write on the image (no source code, selectors, or DOM)
- Where it pastes: Claude (main use case) or any app that accepts a pasted image

## Links

- Home: https://prole.jeffrey.build/
- Download (macOS .dmg): https://prole.jeffrey.build/download/Prole.dmg
- Source: https://github.com/BadMask121/ai-agents
